summary.what_it_does: UIBuilder is a server-driven UI runtime for iOS. It renders native SwiftUI screens entirely from JSON documents, without recompiling the app. A JSON file describes the screen tree, initial state, and event handlers. The app loads, validates, and renders it at runtime.
summary.how_it_works: A JSON file conforming to the 'com.ilya.ui/1.0' schema is loaded from the app bundle by JSONLoader, decoded into a ScreenDocument, validated by DocumentValidator, and then recursively rendered into SwiftUI views by UIRenderer. UIStateStore holds reactive state. User interactions trigger named events which are dispatched via UIActionExecutor to mutate state or navigate. Expressions in visibleWhen/enabledWhen are evaluated by ExpressionEvaluator against live state.

layers.name: App
layers.description: Entry point and root SwiftUI view. Orchestrates loading, validation, state initialization, and rendering.

layers.name: Schema
layers.description: Decodable Swift models that mirror the JSON document structure: ScreenDocument, UINode, UIAction, Expression, DynamicValue.

layers.name: Runtime
layers.description: Execution layer: UIStateStore (reactive state), ExpressionEvaluator (boolean/value expressions), UIActionExecutor (action dispatch), NavigationStore (screen stack), DocumentValidator (schema + tree safety checks).

layers.name: Renderer
layers.description: Translates a UINode tree into SwiftUI views recursively. UIRenderer is the main entry point. NodeStyle applies common layout/visual props. ColorJSON converts hex strings to SwiftUI Color.

layers.name: Support
layers.description: Low-level utilities: DynamicValue (JSON-typed value), JSONLoader (bundle file decoder).

layers.name: Resources
layers.description: JSON files bundled with the app: screen documents and protocol/capability contracts used for authoring and generation guidance.

key_modules.name: UIRenderer
key_modules.role: Recursive SwiftUI view builder from UINode tree

key_modules.name: UIStateStore
key_modules.role: @Observable key-value store driving reactive UI updates

key_modules.name: UIActionExecutor
key_modules.role: Executes action sequences: state mutations, navigation, toasts, AI requests

key_modules.name: ExpressionEvaluator
key_modules.role: Evaluates typed boolean and value expressions against live state

key_modules.name: DocumentValidator
key_modules.role: Validates schema version, node count limit (500), tree depth limit (30), and event reference integrity

key_modules.name: ScreenDocument
key_modules.role: Root decoded model: schema, screenId, state vars, root node tree, events map

key_modules.name: DynamicValue
key_modules.role: Discriminated union for JSON-compatible runtime values: string, number, bool, object, array, null

key_modules.name: Expression
key_modules.role: Recursive expression tree: bind, eq/neq/gt/gte/lt/lte, and/or/not, literal

runtime_flow: 1. App launches → UIBuilderApp creates WindowGroup with RootView
2. RootView.load() calls JSONLoader.loadScreen(named: 'home') → decodes home.json into ScreenDocument
3. DocumentValidator.validate(doc) checks schema version, node count ≤ 500, depth ≤ 30, all event references exist
4. UIStateStore is initialized with doc.state.vars (initial key-value pairs)
5. UIRenderer(node: document.root, ...) is mounted — it walks the UINode tree recursively
6. For each node, UIRenderer checks visibleWhen/enabledWhen via ExpressionEvaluator, then switches on node.type to produce a SwiftUI view
7. NodeStyle.apply() wraps views with padding, frame, background, cornerRadius, opacity modifiers from props
8. User interaction (tap, toggle change) → UIRenderer calls executor.execute(actions, evaluator:)
9. UIActionExecutor iterates actions, checks optional 'when' guard, then dispatches: state.set/inc/dec/toggle, nav.push/pop, ui.toast, ai.request
10. UIStateStore mutation triggers SwiftUI observation → affected views re-render
key_entities: UINode — recursive tree node (type, props, children, events, visibleWhen, enabledWhen)
UIAction — single action descriptor with 'do' field and typed parameters
Expression — recursive expression enum for conditional visibility and enablement
DynamicValue — Codable enum covering all JSON value types
ExpressionEvaluator — stateless evaluator bound to UIStateStore
UIActionExecutor — @MainActor action dispatcher with callback hooks for toast/alert/AI
DocumentValidator — static validator with error enum
UIRenderer — SwiftUI View that recursively renders UINode
NodeStyle — static style applicator returning AnyView
JSONLoader — bundle resource loader and decoder
RootView — app entry SwiftUI view, owns stores and orchestrates the load→validate→render lifecycle
summary.resource_role: JSON files in Resources/ serve two purposes: (1) home.json is a live ScreenDocument loaded and rendered at startup — it is the actual screen definition; (2) full_protocol_example.json is a generation contract / capabilities manifest that describes supported node types, props, actions, strict format rules, and ownership boundaries — intended as a prompt context or reference for authoring valid documents, not loaded at runtime.
summary.current_stage: Early functional prototype. Core runtime is complete and working: schema decoding, validation, recursive rendering, state management, expression evaluation, action execution. Supported node types: vstack, hstack, zstack, scroll, spacer, text, icon, toggle, badge, button. NodeViews.swift is a stub (empty). Navigation is modeled but not yet wired to a NavigationStack in the UI. ai.request and flow.emit actions are defined but not implemented. No unit tests present. ContentView.swift is an unused Xcode template file.
limitations_of_this_analysis: capabilities.json is empty — no content available
full_protocol_example.json read partially (lines 1–200 of 596) — tail section not read but the contract structure is clear from what was read
home.json read partially (lines 1–80 and 450–501) — middle section not read, but structure and event map are confirmed
NodeViews.swift contains only an import statement — no implementation present
UIBuilderApp.swift is a single minified line — fully parsed but no line structure
Assets.xcassets contents.json files not read — standard Xcode asset catalog metadata, not relevant to runtime

tree.purpose: Defines the @main SwiftUI App struct and creates the root WindowGroup with RootView
tree.contains: UIEngineDemoApp: App struct with a single WindowGroup scene containing RootView

tree.purpose: Auto-generated by Xcode project template. Not used anywhere in the actual app flow.
tree.contains: ContentView with a globe image and 'Hello, worldddd!' text. Preview block.

tree.purpose: Top-level SwiftUI app layer: root view that owns state and orchestrates the full load → validate → render lifecycle
Loads home.json, validates it, initializes UIStateStore, and mounts UIRenderer. Also wires toast callbacks on UIActionExecutor.
tree.contains: RootView: View with @State document, stateStore, navigationStore. Async load() using JSONLoader + DocumentValidator. Conditional rendering: ProgressView while loading, UIRenderer when ready. Alert presenter for toast messages. configured(_:) method wiring onToast and onAIRequest closures.

tree.purpose: Core engine: all schema models, runtime execution, and rendering logic
Decodable Swift models that represent the JSON document protocol
Defines the root decoded type for a screen JSON file and its sub-documents
tree.contains: ScreenDocument (schema, screenId, title, theme, state, root: UINode, events, meta). ThemeTokens (spacing, cornerRadius, colors, fonts). StateDocument (vars: [String: DynamicValue]). MetaDocument (version, author, tags).

tree.purpose: Represents one node in the recursive UI tree decoded from JSON
tree.contains: UINode (id, type, props: [String: DynamicValue], children: [UINode], visibleWhen: Expression, enabledWhen: Expression, event: [String: String], items: [ListItem]). ListItem (id, title, event). stableId computed property.

tree.purpose: Decoded model for a single action in an event handler array
tree.contains: UIAction (action via CodingKey 'do', key, value, min, max, screenId, message, title, event, payload, id, input, when: Expression). All fields optional except 'do'.

tree.purpose: Represents a recursive boolean/value expression used in visibleWhen and enabledWhen node props
tree.contains: Expression: indirect enum (bind, eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, and, or, not, literal). Custom Decodable supporting object-syntax {"bind": ...}, operator arrays, and scalar literal fallback. ExpressionValue enum (expression | literal). ExpressionPayload helper struct for decoding.

tree.purpose: Execution layer: reactive state, expression evaluation, action dispatch, navigation, document validation
Holds the live key-value state for a screen, driving SwiftUI observation and re-renders on mutation
tree.contains: @Observable UIStateStore with values: [String: DynamicValue]. Methods: get, set, increment(max:), decrement(min:), toggle.

tree.purpose: Evaluates Expression trees to Bool or DynamicValue by reading live state from UIStateStore
tree.contains: ExpressionEvaluator struct (state: UIStateStore). evaluateBool(_:) recursive switch over all Expression cases. resolveValue(_:) for value resolution. Private compare() and compareNumbers() helpers. Private ComparisonValue enum for type-safe equality/ordering.

tree.purpose: Iterates an array of UIActions and executes each against UIStateStore or NavigationStore, with callback hooks for side effects
tree.contains: @MainActor UIActionExecutor. Callback properties: onToast, onAlert, onAIRequest. execute(_:evaluator:) iterates actions, checks optional 'when' guard via ExpressionEvaluator, dispatches: state.set/inc/dec/toggle, nav.push/pop, ui.toast, ui.alert, flow.emit (no-op), ai.request.

tree.purpose: Tracks the current navigation stack as an ordered array of screenId strings
tree.contains: @Observable NavigationStore. path: [String]. push(_:) and pop() methods.

tree.purpose: Validates a decoded ScreenDocument before rendering: schema version, node tree size, depth, and event reference integrity
tree.contains: DocumentValidationError enum (missingSchema, unsupportedSchema, tooManyNodes, treeTooDeep, missingEvent). DocumentValidator struct with static validate(_:). Checks schema == 'com.ilya.ui/1.0', node count ≤ 500, depth ≤ 30, all node event references exist in document.events map.

tree.purpose: SwiftUI rendering layer: translates UINode trees into views and applies visual styling
Core rendering entry point. Takes a UINode and recursively produces SwiftUI views based on node.type
tree.contains: UIRenderer: View. Checks visibleWhen and enabledWhen via ExpressionEvaluator. renderedNode(evaluator:) switches on node.type: vstack, hstack, zstack, scroll, spacer, text, icon, button, toggle, badge. Unsupported types show fallback text. trigger() dispatches events. resolveText() handles literal, bind, and format+binds text values. alignment() and font() helpers.

tree.purpose: Applies common layout and visual props from a UINode.props dictionary to any SwiftUI view
tree.contains: NodeStyle struct with static apply<V: View>(_:props:) -> AnyView. Applies: padding, width/height, minWidth/minHeight, maxWidth/maxHeight (supports 'infinity'), opacity, cornerRadius (via clipShape RoundedRectangle), backgroundColor, foregroundColor.

tree.purpose: Extends SwiftUI Color with hex string initialization for use in NodeStyle
tree.contains: Color extension with init(hex: String) and static fromDynamic(_: DynamicValue?) -> Color?. Parses 6-digit hex strings with optional '#' prefix into RGB components.

tree.responsibility: Reserved for custom node view components
tree.purpose: Placeholder file — currently empty (import only). Intended for dedicated SwiftUI View structs per node type.
tree.contains: import SwiftUI only. No types defined.

tree.purpose: Low-level shared utilities used across Schema, Runtime, and Renderer
Universal value type for all runtime data flowing through the engine: props, state, action values, expression literals
tree.contains: DynamicValue: Codable, Equatable, Sendable enum with cases: string(String), number(Double), bool(Bool), object([String: DynamicValue]), array([DynamicValue]), null. Custom Decodable with Bool-before-Double priority. Accessors: stringValue, doubleValue, boolValue.

tree.purpose: Locates a named JSON file in the app bundle and decodes it into a ScreenDocument
tree.contains: JSONLoader enum with static loadScreen(named:) throws -> ScreenDocument. Uses Bundle.main.url(forResource:withExtension:) and JSONDecoder.

tree.purpose: JSON files bundled with the app: live screen documents and protocol contracts for document authoring
The actual screen loaded and rendered at app startup. Defines a 'Controls' screen with toggle rows (Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, Notifications, Location), icon buttons, and a status label.
tree.contains: schema: 'com.ilya.ui/1.0', screenId: 'controls-selection-screen'. State vars: selectedField (string), wifiEnabled, bluetoothEnabled, notificationsEnabled, locationEnabled (bools). Root: scroll > vstack with icon button row (hstack) and settings rows (each hstack with button + spacer + toggle). Events: homePressed, searchPressed, bellPressed, gearPressed, wifiRowPressed, wifiChanged, bluetoothRowPressed, bluetoothChanged, notificationsRowPressed, notificationsChanged, locationRowPressed, locationChanged — all use state.set to update selectedField.

tree.purpose: Intended to declare engine capabilities for tooling or agents. Currently empty.
tree.contains: Empty file — no content.

tree.purpose: Strict authoring contract for generating valid ScreenDocuments. Describes supported node types, props, actions, strict format rules, and ownership boundaries between app and JSON.
tree.contains: version: 2, status: 'base-stable-strict'. Engine metadata. Supported nodeTypes: layout (vstack, hstack, zstack, scroll, spacer), primitive (text, icon, toggle, badge), composite (button, card). Per-node childrenAllowed and requiredProps rules. Common props list. strictFormats for event wiring, action format, text value formats. Supported actions: state.set/inc/dec/toggle, ui.toast, ai.request. Disallowed node types: frame, box, columns, toolbar. ownershipBoundaries: app owns shell/safe area/layout policy; JSON owns content tree/state bindings/event wiring. generationRules list.
